title: Ex-RAF test pilot says Chinook crashed on safety 'show flight'

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Four crew and 25 passengers were killed when an RAF Chinook struck a hillside in foggy conditions over the Mull of Kintyre in 1994.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Four crew and 25 passengers were killed when the helicopter crashed in June 1994 A Chinook helicopter that crashed in Scotland more than 30 years ago, killing 29 people, was being flown as a "show flight" after safety concerns were raised about its airworthiness, a former RAF test pilot has said.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: Four crew and 25 passengers were killed when RAF Chinook ZD576 struck a hillside in foggy conditions over the Mull of Kintyre in 1994. Retired Squadron Leader Robert Burke said the Mark 2 version of the Chinook had been chosen to demonstrate to the Army that a recent upgrade from the Mark 1 version was safe.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Ex-RAF test pilot says Chinook crashed on 'show flight'
